name: Wonston
overview: Wonston is a small village on the chalk-stream River Dever about 5 miles north of Winchester. It gives its name to the wider civil parish, which also takes in Sutton Scotney, Hunton, Stoke Charity and Norton; most of the parish's residents actually live in neighbouring Sutton Scotney. The village sits on the northern slope of the downland ridge that rises north-west of Winchester, in quiet farming country.
character: A quiet, linear chalk-stream village of cottages and farms strung along the Dever valley, away from main roads.
amenities: Parish church and village community life
Everyday shops, services and facilities in adjoining Sutton Scotney
No shop or pub in Wonston itself

commutes.notes: Nearest station, with direct trains to London Waterloo (fastest about 1h 2m)
About 5 miles south for city shops, services and fast trains; the A34 and M3 are close by
Direct from Micheldever on South Western Railway

prices.summary: Housing is mainly period cottages and detached village houses with some farms, in low transaction volumes; the quiet Dever-valley position and easy commute support solid values. See the live Rightmove sold-price page for current figures.

thingsToDo: Walking along the River Dever and over the downland ridge towards Winchester
Exploring the wider Wonston parish villages and Sutton Scotney
Country drives to Micheldever and the Test valley
placesToEat: Pubs and shops in adjoining Sutton Scotney
pros: Quiet, rural chalk-stream village close to Winchester
Good road and rail links via the A34/M3 and nearby Micheldever station
Fuller amenities on hand in adjoining Sutton Scotney
cons: Few amenities in Wonston village itself
Car needed for shops, schools and the station
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a quiet Dever-valley village within easy reach of Winchester and a fast London commute, with everyday shops next door in Sutton Scotney.
